content:
Complete the given **GroupBooking** class by adding new features by following the given instructions.

**Existing `GroupBooking` class**:

The existing **GroupBooking** class inherits from the **Booking** class but contains only a `pass` statement. Remove the `pass` statement and add the new features as mentioned in the given instructions.

<details>

<summary>**Features to be added to **`GroupBooking`** class**</summary>

- **Add below attributes**:
 - The **GroupBooking** class should have the following additional attribute:
  - **`group_size`** (an integer representing the number of passengers in the group)

- **Implement the following methods**:
 - `add_group_member()`: This method should accept a name and add it to the **passengers** list attribute from the **Booking** class.
  - Method should check if the number of passengers in the group is less than the maximum allowed group size.
  - If the **group size is full**, it will display the message **Cannot add more passengers, group is full.**
 - `view_group()`: This method should print all the names of passengers in the group each on a new line.
  -  The method should print the group details in the below format

    ```
    Group members:
    #  Name of the Group member each in a new Line
    ```  
</details>


<details>

<summary>**Features to be added to **`CustomGroupBooking`** class**</summary>


The **CustomGroupBooking** class **inherits from the GroupBooking class**. Add the following additional features:

- **Add below attributes**:
 - The **GroupBooking** class should have the following additional attribute:
  - **`group_name`**: A string representing the name of the group.

- **Implement the following methods**:
 - `get_group_details()`: This method should prints detailed information about the group, as given below :

    ```
    Group Name: {group_name}
    Booking ID: {bookingid}
    Flight: {flight}
    Status: {status}
    Group Size: {group_size}
    Passengers:
    # Name of each passenger, one per line

    ```  
</details>

<MultiLineNote>

- Do not edit the `default_test` function as this will effect the test cases.
- The output of the testcase **Checking Default Tests** is given by the `default_test` function in the prefilled code.
- This coding question does not have the usual input/output testcases. The class defined by you will be tested internally to check whether the attributes are present or not. The test case results will provide a rough description of the tests being verified.
</MultiLineNote>

---

test_cases.input: Checking if correct attribute names are used
test_cases.output: PASS: All attributes are present in the GroupBooking and CustomGroupBooking classes.

test_cases.input: Checking if methods are implemented correctly
test_cases.output: PASS: All methods are implemented correctly in the GroupBooking and CustomGroupBooking classes.

Group members:
Alice
Bob
Charlie
Group Size: 5
Group Name: Team Alpha
Booking ID: CB123
Flight: FL789
Status: Pending
Group Size: 3
Passengers:
David
Eve
